differential_diagnoses: Cardiac metastasis from occult solid tumor (e.g., lung or kidney)
Right atrial thrombus with secondary infectious or inflammatory involvement
next_steps: Perform EBUS-TBNA of mediastinal lymph nodes to establish a tissue diagnosis safely
Obtain cardiac magnetic resonance (CMR) imaging for improved tissue characterization
Proceed with PET/CT scan to assess systemic tumor burden and identify optimal biopsy sites
Optimize anticoagulation management given presence of pulmonary emboli
Consult cardiothoracic surgery for consideration of surgical biopsy or resection if feasible
Perform CT of the abdomen and pelvis to screen for potential extracardiac primary malignancy
